section_name: Erecting Off-premise Sign Without License; Offense
chapter_title: Regul. of Outdoor Signs on Rural Roads
text:
(a) A person commits an offense if the person wilfully erects or maintains an off-premise sign on a rural road without a license under this subchapter.

(b) An offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $500 or more than $1,000. Each day of the proscribed conduct is a separate offense.

(c) A person is not required to obtain a license to erect or maintain an on-premise sign.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 394. Regul. of Outdoor Signs on Rural Roads
##### § 394.0201 — Erecting Off-premise Sign Without License; Offense

(a) A person commits an offense if the person wilfully erects or maintains an off-premise sign on a rural road without a license under this subchapter.

(b) An offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $500 or more than $1,000. Each day of the proscribed conduct is a separate offense.

(c) A person is not required to obtain a license to erect or maintain an on-premise sign.

*Added by Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1345 (S.B.1420), Sec. 49(a), eff. September 1, 2011.*

subsections.text: An offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $500 or more than $1,000. Each day of the proscribed conduct is a separate offense.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: A person is not required to obtain a license to erect or maintain an on-premise sign.
